seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Agentic RAG with LangGraph enables sophisticated multi-step retrieval agents that plan queries, fetch from diverse sources, and synthesize attributed responses using stateful graph workflows. These complex, cyclical agents excel at handling dynamic reasoning but introduce risks like infinite loops, state corruption, and hallucinated attributions. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness, query accuracy, and consistency in high-stakes deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangGraph Agentic RAG agent is structured as a stateful graph with nodes for query planning, multi-source retrieval, reranking, synthesis, and citation generation. The agent maintains a shared state schema including messages, retrieved chunks, planned queries, and attribution metadata. Edges use conditional routing to decide between parallel retrieval branches, human review, or final synthesis based on confidence scores and state conditions.

The workflow begins with a planner node that decomposes user queries into sub-queries using an LLM. Parallel retriever nodes then query vector stores, web search, and knowledge bases simultaneously. A grader node assesses chunk relevance and source consistency before passing to a synthesizer that generates cited responses. Checkpoints persist state across interruptions, enabling human-in-the-loop interventions at configurable breakpoints.

This cyclical design allows agents to self-correct by re-planning poor queries or merging conflicting retrievals, but requires robust state management and verification to prevent cascading errors.

risks.name: Cyclic query re-planning loops
risks.description: Agent endlessly re-plans queries due to low confidence scores without termination conditions
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors state transition counts and verifies planning logic terminates

risks.name: Cross-source attribution hallucination
risks.description: Synthesis node fabricates citations mixing chunks from unrelated sources
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies citation traceability to actual retrieved chunks

risks.name: Parallel branch state conflicts
risks.description: Merging retrievals from vector DB vs web search overwrites attribution metadata
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation ensures consistent state updates across branches

risks.name: Bypassed human review breakpoints
risks.description: Conditional edges skip human-in-loop nodes during high-confidence paths
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces breakpoint execution regardless of routing decisions

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Catch infinite loops and state corruption at workflow boundaries
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-key')

# Your compiled LangGraph
rag_graph = StateGraph(AgentState)
# ... add nodes and edges ...
graph = rag_graph.compile(checkpointer=memory)

# Wrap invocation
@vex.verify(
    guards=[
        'no-infinite-loops',
        'state-schema-valid',
        'breakpoint-enforced'
    ],
    timeout='30s'
)
def verified_invoke(input_state: AgentState):
    return graph.invoke(input_state)

result = verified_invoke({
    'messages': ['What is Agentic RAG?'],
    'planned_queries': [],
    'retrieved_chunks': [],
    'citations': [],
    'confidence': 0.0
})

vexIntegration.title: Vex Node for Source Faithfulness
Query Planning Accuracy Guard
vexIntegration.description: Ensure planned sub-queries are relevant to original question
vexIntegration.code:
def vex_planner_guard(state: AgentState) -> AgentState:
    original_query = state['messages'][0]
    planned = state['planned_queries']
    
    # Verify planning relevance
    planning_check = vex_client.verify(
        claim=f"Queries {{planned}} answer {{original_query}}",
        checks=['query-planning-accuracy'],
        context={
            'original': original_query,
            'planned': planned
        }
    )
    
    if planning_check.score < 0.8:
        raise ValueError(f'Poor planning: {planning_check.reason}')
    
    state['planning_verified'] = True
    return state

# Add as node before parallel retrieval
rag_graph.add_node('vex_planner', vex_planner_guard)
